_comment: Domain-surface projection config for `langium-zod generate --domain`.
FULL, LOSSLESS, INFORMATION-COMPLETE surface (Phase 3D, constraint 4b):
- NO per-type `fields` whitelist — every grammar-authored field is kept,
including ones the FORM surface (form-surfaces.json) drops (typeCallArgs,
references, enumSynonyms, synonyms, annotations, conditions, postConditions,
operations, RosettaFunction.superFunction, ...).
- Only `$`-internals are stripped, via the `--strip-internals` CLI flag
($container/$containerProperty/$containerIndex/$cstNode/$document/$refNode/$nodeDescription).
- Cross-refs project to branded `Ref<'Target'>`; `$type` retained as the
discriminant (both from the emitter, Phase 3D-1).
- Expressions are projected FULLY (decision 2026-06-08): the emitter's
inline-union read projection (domainReadExpr union arm) handles `typeRef:
RosettaSuperCall | RosettaSymbolReference` and the rest of the expression
tree via the master `toDomain` dispatch. No exclude.
Normalizations (constraint 4c) — additive, read-only, lossless canonical views:
inheritance → `extends`: unifies Data.superType / RosettaFunction.superFunction /
RosettaEnumeration.parent (each aliases its source's projected Ref<T>).
members → `members`: unifies attributes / enumValues / inputs / features
(each aliases its source's projected element-Domain[]; cross-kind union on AnyDomain).
`from` values are the POST-rename domain field names (no renames here, so == AST names).
